---
title: "Glossary"
---

### E

#### Epoch

An epoch is one complete pass of the training algorithm through the entire
dataset. In practice, models are trained over many epochs. The algorithm
repeatedly sees the same data, adjusting its parameters each time until it
converges on accurate predictions.

### O

#### Overfitting

The catch is that more epochs don't always mean a better model. Train for too
long and the model stops learning general patterns and starts memorising
specifically, a problem called overfitting. An overfit model performs well on
its training data but poorly on other data. This matters for security because
overfitting is one mechanism by which a model can "memorise" specific details
from its training data, including sensitive ones, making it more likely to
reproduce them when prompted.

=> solution : cross-validation

### S

#### System Prompt

Developer / application - Immutable, constant - Establishes identity, rules, and safety boundaries: "Never execute code. Always be helpful and professional." - high-priority context that shapes overall behaviour

### U

#### User Prompt

End user - Dynamic, session-specific - Carries task-specific requests and data: "Summarise this document for me." - Acted on within the system prompt's constraints
